values.title: Modular Architecture
values.description: Build with composable plugins and extensions. Add only what you need and customize everything.

values.title: Community Driven
values.description: Over 500 contributors worldwide. Every pull request is reviewed and every voice matters.

values.title: Security First
values.description: Regular security audits, automated vulnerability scanning, and responsible disclosure policy.

values.title: Blazing Performance
values.description: Optimized for speed with lazy loading, tree shaking, and zero-cost abstractions.

values.title: Comprehensive Docs
values.description: Detailed guides, API references, and tutorials to get you productive quickly.

values.title: MIT Licensed
values.description: Use it freely in commercial and personal projects. No strings attached.

values.title: OpenForge Project Homepage
values.hero_description: OpenForge is an open-source framework that empowers developers to create robust, scalable applications. Join thousands of contributors shaping the future of software.
values.features_subtitle: Built by developers, for developers. Every feature exists because the community needed it.
values.cta_description: Whether you're fixing a typo or building a new module, every contribution makes OpenForge better. Star us on GitHub and get started today.

values.title: Plugin System
values.body:
OpenForge's plugin system allows you to extend core functionality without modifying the source code. Plugins hook into lifecycle events and can add routes, middleware, data transformations, and UI components.

How It Works

Plugins are standard npm packages that export a configuration object. The plugin loader handles dependency resolution, ordering, and conflict detection automatically.

Creating a Plugin

Use the CLI scaffolding tool to generate a plugin template with tests, documentation, and CI configuration pre-configured.

values.title: CLI Developer Tools
values.body:
The OpenForge CLI provides a powerful set of developer tools for scaffolding projects, generating components, running tests, and deploying applications.

Key Commands

  • forge init - Scaffold a new project with best practices
  • forge generate - Create components, services, and plugins
  • forge test - Run unit and integration tests
  • forge deploy - Deploy to any cloud provider

values.title: GraphQL API Layer
values.body:
OpenForge includes a built-in GraphQL API layer that automatically generates schemas from your data models. Query exactly what you need with type safety and real-time subscriptions.

Auto-Generated Schemas

Define your models and OpenForge generates the full GraphQL schema including queries, mutations, and subscriptions.

Playground Included

An interactive GraphQL playground ships with every project for easy API exploration and testing.

values.title: Built-in Testing Framework
values.body:
Ship with confidence using OpenForge's integrated testing framework. Unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end tests are first-class citizens.

Test Utilities

Mock providers, fixture generators, and snapshot testing come out of the box. Write tests that are readable and maintainable.

CI Integration

Pre-configured GitHub Actions workflows run your entire test suite on every pull request.

values.title: Sarah Chen
values.body:
Sarah is the original creator and lead maintainer of OpenForge. She started the project in 2021 while working on distributed systems at a major tech company. Her vision was to create a framework that makes complex architectures accessible to every developer.

She focuses on the core runtime, plugin system, and long-term architecture decisions. Sarah regularly speaks at conferences about open source sustainability and community building.

values.title: Marcus Johnson
values.body:
Marcus leads the CLI tooling and developer experience efforts. Before contributing to OpenForge, he built developer tools at several startups and learned firsthand what makes a great development workflow.

His contributions include the scaffolding system, the interactive configuration wizard, and the hot-reload development server. Marcus also mentors new contributors through the onboarding program.

values.title: Elena Rodriguez
values.body:
Elena is the security lead for OpenForge. She conducts security audits, manages the vulnerability disclosure program, and ensures every release meets strict security standards.

With a background in application security and penetration testing, Elena has hardened the framework against common attack vectors and established automated security scanning in the CI pipeline.

values.title: Akira Tanaka
values.body:
Akira maintains the documentation, tutorials, and learning resources for OpenForge. He believes that great documentation is just as important as great code.

He has written over 200 pages of guides, built the interactive tutorial system, and translates documentation into Japanese. Akira also manages the community Discord server and weekly office hours.

values.title: OpenForge v3.0.0 - Catalyst
values.body:
OpenForge v3.0.0 "Catalyst" is a major release introducing the new reactive state management system, improved plugin API, and a complete rewrite of the build pipeline.

Highlights

  • New reactive state management with fine-grained reactivity
  • Plugin API v2 with lifecycle hooks and dependency injection
  • 50% faster build times with the new bundler
  • TypeScript 5.3 support with decorator metadata

Migration Guide

The migration guide covers all breaking changes and provides codemods for automated migration of most patterns.

values.title: OpenForge v2.8.0 - Steady State
values.body:
Version 2.8.0 focuses on stability, performance improvements, and quality-of-life features requested by the community.

What's New

  • Improved error messages with source location tracking
  • New debug panel for visualizing component trees
  • Reduced memory usage by 30% in large applications
  • Added support for streaming server-side rendering

values.title: OpenForge v2.7.0 - Signal Boost
values.body:
Version 2.7.0 introduces the signals API for fine-grained reactivity and adds first-class support for edge runtime deployments.

Key Changes

  • Signals API for reactive primitives
  • Edge runtime adapter for Cloudflare Workers and Deno Deploy
  • New testing utilities for async operations
  • Improved TypeScript inference for plugin configurations

values.title: About OpenForge
values.body:
OpenForge is an open-source framework created in 2021 with the mission of making complex software architectures accessible to every developer. What started as a side project has grown into a thriving community of over 500 contributors from 40+ countries.

Our Mission

We believe that the best software is built in the open. Every line of code, every design decision, and every roadmap item is transparent and community-driven. We are committed to creating tools that are free, secure, and well-documented.

Governance

OpenForge is governed by a core team of elected maintainers and an advisory board. Major decisions are made through RFCs (Request for Comments) where any community member can participate.

Sponsorship

OpenForge is funded through GitHub Sponsors, Open Collective, and corporate sponsorships. All financial records are public and auditable.

values.title: Contributing Guide
values.body:
Thank you for your interest in contributing to OpenForge! We welcome contributions of all kinds: code, documentation, bug reports, feature requests, and community support.

Getting Started

  1. Fork the repository on GitHub
  2. Clone your fork locally
  3. Run forge setup to install dependencies
  4. Create a feature branch from main
  5. Make your changes with tests
  6. Submit a pull request

Code of Conduct

We are committed to providing a welcoming and inclusive experience for everyone. Please read our Code of Conduct before participating.

First-Time Contributors

Look for issues labeled good-first-issue for beginner-friendly tasks. Our mentoring program pairs new contributors with experienced maintainers.
